001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassArrayFormat.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassNameFormat.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
028
029import java.lang.annotation.*;
030import java.util.*;
031import java.util.function.*;
032
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
035
036/**
037 * Encapsulates information about an annotation instance and the element it's declared on.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around Java annotations that allows you to:
041 * <ul>
042 *    <li>Access annotation values in a type-safe manner
043 *    <li>Query annotation properties without reflection boilerplate
044 *    <li>Track where the annotation was found (class, method, field, etc.)
045 *    <li>Sort annotations by precedence using ranks
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
049 * <p class='bjava'>
050 *    <jc>// Get annotation info from a class</jc>
051 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
052 *    Optional&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>ai</jv> =
053 *       <jv>ci</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).findFirst();
054 *
055 *    <jc>// Access annotation values</jc>
056 *    <jv>ai</jv>.ifPresent(<jv>x</jv> -&gt; {
057 *       String <jv>value</jv> = <jv>x</jv>.getValue(String.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"value"</js>).orElse(<js>"default"</js>);
058 *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>priority</jv> = <jv>x</jv>.getInt(<js>"priority"</js>).orElse(0);
059 *    });
060 * </p>
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo}
064 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo}
065 *    <li class='jc'>{@link FieldInfo}
066 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo}
067 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ParameterInfo}
068 *    <li class='jc'>{@link PackageInfo}
069 * </ul>
070 *
071 * @param <T> The annotation type.
072 */
073@SuppressWarnings({
074   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
075   "java:S3740"  // Raw Annotation type used intentionally for unparameterized annotation introspection
076})
077public class AnnotationInfo<T extends Annotation> {
078
079   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
080   private static final String ARG_on = "on";
081   private static final String ARG_a = "a";
082
083   /**
084    * Creates a new annotation info object.
085    *
086    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
087    * <p class='bjava'>
088    *    <jc>// Create annotation info for a class annotation</jc>
089    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
090    *    MyAnnotation <jv>annotation</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.inner().getAnnotation(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>);
091    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = AnnotationInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>ci</jv>, <jv>annotation</jv>);
092    * </p>
093    *
094    * @param <A> The annotation type.
095    * @param on The annotatable object where the annotation was found (class, method, field, constructor, parameter, or package).
096    * @param value The annotation instance. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
097    * @return A new {@link AnnotationInfo} object wrapping the annotation.
098    */
099   public static <A extends Annotation> AnnotationInfo<A> of(Annotatable on, A value) {
100      return new AnnotationInfo<>(on, value);
101   }
102
103   private static int findRank(Object a) {
104      // @formatter:off
105      return ClassInfo.of(a).getAllMethods().stream()
106         .filter(m -> m.hasName("rank") && m.hasReturnType(int.class))
107         .findFirst()
108         .map(m -> safe(() -> (int)m.invoke(a)))
109         .orElse(0);
110      // @formatter:on
111   }
112
113   private final Annotatable annotatable;
114   final int rank;
115
116   private T a;  // Effectively final
117
118   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> methods = memoize(() -> stream(a.annotationType().getMethods()).map(m -> MethodInfo.of(info(a.annotationType()), m)).toList());
119   private final Supplier<String> toString;  // String representation with annotation type, location, and values.
120
121   /**
122    * Constructor.
123    *
124    * @param on The annotatable object where the annotation was found.
125    * @param a The annotation instance.
126    */
127   AnnotationInfo(Annotatable on, T a) {
128      this.annotatable = assertArgNotNull(ARG_on, on);
129      this.a = assertArgNotNull(ARG_a, a);
130      this.rank = findRank(a);
131      this.toString = memoize(this::findToString);
132   }
133
134   /**
135    * Returns the annotatable object (class, method, field, etc.) where this annotation was found.
136    *
137    * @return The annotatable object where this annotation was found.
138    */
139   public Annotatable getAnnotatable() {
140      return annotatable;
141   }
142
143   /**
144    * Returns the annotatable object cast to the specified type.
145    *
146    * @param <A> The annotatable type.
147    * @param type The class to cast to.
148    * @return The annotatable element cast to the specified type.
149    * @throws ClassCastException If the annotatable element is not of the specified type.
150    */
151   public <A extends Annotatable> A getAnnotatable(Class<A> type) {
152      return type.cast(annotatable);
153   }
154
155   /**
156    * Returns the annotation type of this annotation.
157    *
158    * <p>
159    * Same as calling {@link Annotation#annotationType()}.
160    *
161    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
162    * <p class='bjava'>
163    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;Deprecated&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getAnnotation(Deprecated.<jk>class</jk>);
164    *    Class&lt;? <jk>extends</jk> Annotation&gt; <jv>type</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.annotationType();  <jc>// Returns Deprecated.class</jc>
165    * </p>
166    *
167    * @return The annotation type of this annotation.
168    * @see Annotation#annotationType()
169    */
170   public Class<? extends Annotation> annotationType() {
171      return a.annotationType();
172   }
173
174   /**
175    * Casts this annotation info to a specific annotation type.
176    *
177    * <p>
178    * This is useful when you have an {@code AnnotationInfo<?>} and need to narrow it to a specific type.
179    *
180    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
181    * <p class='bjava'>
182    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;?&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
183    *
184    *    <jc>// Safe cast</jc>
185    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>myAi</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.cast(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>);
186    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>myAi</jv> != <jk>null</jk>) {
187    *       <jc>// Use strongly-typed annotation info</jc>
188    *    }
189    * </p>
190    *
191    * @param <A> The annotation type to cast to.
192    * @param type The annotation type to cast to.
193    * @return This annotation info cast to the specified type, or <jk>null</jk> if the annotation is not of the specified type.
194    */
195   @SuppressWarnings({
196      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to AnnotationInfo<A>
197   })
198   public <A extends Annotation> AnnotationInfo<A> cast(Class<A> type) {
199      return type.isInstance(a) ? (AnnotationInfo<A>)this : null;
200   }
201
202   /**
203    * Returns true if the specified object represents an annotation that is logically equivalent to this one.
204    *
205    * <p>
206    * Same as calling {@link Annotation#equals(Object)} on the wrapped annotation.
207    *
208    * <p>
209    * Two annotations are considered equal if:
210    * <ul>
211    *    <li>They are of the same annotation type
212    *    <li>All their corresponding member values are equal
213    * </ul>
214    *
215    * @param o The reference object with which to compare.
216    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is equal to this annotation.
217    * @see Annotation#equals(Object)
218    */
219   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
220   public boolean equals(Object o) {
221      if (o instanceof AnnotationInfo o2)
222         return a.equals(o2.a);
223      return a.equals(o);
224   }
225
226   /**
227    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a boolean.
228    *
229    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
230    * <p class='bjava'>
231    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(enabled=true)</jc>
232    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
233    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>enabled</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getBoolean(<js>"enabled"</js>).orElse(<jk>false</jk>);  <jc>// Returns true</jc>
234    * </p>
235    *
236    * @param methodName The method name.
237    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as a boolean, or empty if not found or not a {@code boolean} type.
238    */
239   public Optional<Boolean> getBoolean(String methodName) {
240      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(boolean.class)).map(x -> (Boolean)x.invoke(a));
241   }
242
243   /**
244    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a class array.
245    *
246    * <p>
247    * For type-safe access to an array of classes of a specific supertype, use {@link #getClassArray(String, Class)}.
248    *
249    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
250    * <p class='bjava'>
251    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(types={String.class, Integer.class})</jc>
252    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
253    *    Class&lt;?&gt;[] <jv>types</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getClassArray(<js>"types"</js>).orElse(<jk>new</jk> Class[0]);  <jc>// Returns [String.class, Integer.class]</jc>
254    * </p>
255    *
256    * @param methodName The method name.
257    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the class array value, or empty if not found or not a {@code Class[]} type.
258    */
259   @SuppressWarnings({
260      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Class<?>[] for annotation value
261   })
262   public Optional<Class<?>[]> getClassArray(String methodName) {
263      return (Optional<Class<?>[]>)(Optional<?>)getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(Class[].class)).map(x -> x.invoke(a));
264   }
265
266   /**
267    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a class array of a specific type.
268    *
269    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
270    * <p class='bjava'>
271    *    <jc>// Get an array of serializer classes from an annotation</jc>
272    *    Optional&lt;Class&lt;? <jk>extends</jk> Serializer&gt;[]&gt; <jv>serializerClasses</jv> =
273    *       <jv>annotationInfo</jv>.getClassArray(<js>"serializers"</js>, Serializer.<jk>class</jk>);
274    * </p>
275    *
276    * @param <T> The expected supertype of the classes.
277    * @param methodName The method name.
278    * @param type The expected supertype of the class values.
279    * @return An optional containing the value of the specified method cast to the expected type,
280    *         or empty if not found, not a class array, or any element is not assignable to the expected type.
281    */
282   @SuppressWarnings({
283      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
284      "hiding", // Field/variable hiding intentional for specific scoping
285   })
286   public <T> Optional<Class<? extends T>[]> getClassArray(String methodName, Class<T> type) {
287      // @formatter:off
288      return getMethod(methodName)
289         .filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(Class[].class))
290         .map(x -> (Class<?>[])x.invoke(a))
291         .filter(arr -> {
292            for (var c : arr) {
293               if (!type.isAssignableFrom(c))
294                  return false;
295            }
296            return true;
297         })
298         .map(x -> (Class<? extends T>[])x);
299      // @formatter:on
300   }
301
302   /**
303    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a class.
304    *
305    * <p>
306    * For type-safe access to a class of a specific supertype, use {@link #getClassValue(String, Class)}.
307    *
308    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
309    * <p class='bjava'>
310    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(type=String.class)</jc>
311    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
312    *    Class&lt;?&gt; <jv>type</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getClassValue(<js>"type"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);  <jc>// Returns String.class</jc>
313    * </p>
314    *
315    * @param methodName The method name.
316    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the class value, or empty if not found or not a {@link Class} type.
317    */
318   @SuppressWarnings({
319      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Class<?> for annotation value
320   })
321   public Optional<Class<?>> getClassValue(String methodName) {
322      return (Optional<Class<?>>)(Optional<?>)getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(Class.class)).map(x -> x.invoke(a));
323   }
324
325   /**
326    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a class of a specific type.
327    *
328    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
329    * <p class='bjava'>
330    *    <jc>// Get a serializer class from an annotation</jc>
331    *    Optional&lt;Class&lt;? <jk>extends</jk> Serializer&gt;&gt; <jv>serializerClass</jv> =
332    *       <jv>annotationInfo</jv>.getClassValue(<js>"serializer"</js>, Serializer.<jk>class</jk>);
333    * </p>
334    *
335    * @param <T> The expected supertype of the class.
336    * @param methodName The method name.
337    * @param type The expected supertype of the class value.
338    * @return An optional containing the value of the specified method cast to the expected type,
339    *         or empty if not found, not a class, or not assignable to the expected type.
340    */
341   @SuppressWarnings({
342      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
343      "hiding", // Field/variable hiding intentional for specific scoping
344   })
345   public <T> Optional<Class<? extends T>> getClassValue(String methodName, Class<T> type) {
346      // @formatter:off
347      return getMethod(methodName)
348         .filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(Class.class))
349         .map(x -> (Class<?>)x.invoke(a))
350         .filter(type::isAssignableFrom)
351         .map(x -> (Class<? extends T>)x);
352      // @formatter:on
353   }
354
355   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
356   // Annotation interface methods
357   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
358
359   /**
360    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a double.
361    *
362    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
363    * <p class='bjava'>
364    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(threshold=0.95)</jc>
365    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
366    *    <jk>double</jk> <jv>threshold</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getDouble(<js>"threshold"</js>).orElse(0.0);  <jc>// Returns 0.95</jc>
367    * </p>
368    *
369    * @param methodName The method name.
370    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as a double, or empty if not found or not a {@code double} type.
371    */
372   public Optional<Double> getDouble(String methodName) {
373      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(double.class)).map(x -> (Double)x.invoke(a));
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a float.
378    *
379    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
380    * <p class='bjava'>
381    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(weight=0.5f)</jc>
382    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
383    *    <jk>float</jk> <jv>weight</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getFloat(<js>"weight"</js>).orElse(0.0f);  <jc>// Returns 0.5f</jc>
384    * </p>
385    *
386    * @param methodName The method name.
387    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as a float, or empty if not found or not a {@code float} type.
388    */
389   public Optional<Float> getFloat(String methodName) {
390      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(float.class)).map(x -> (Float)x.invoke(a));
391   }
392
393   /**
394    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as an integer.
395    *
396    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
397    * <p class='bjava'>
398    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(priority=5)</jc>
399    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
400    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>priority</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getInt(<js>"priority"</js>).orElse(0);  <jc>// Returns 5</jc>
401    * </p>
402    *
403    * @param methodName The method name.
404    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as an integer, or empty if not found or not an {@code int} type.
405    */
406   public Optional<Integer> getInt(String methodName) {
407      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(int.class)).map(x -> (Integer)x.invoke(a));
408   }
409
410   /**
411    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a long.
412    *
413    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
414    * <p class='bjava'>
415    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(timestamp=1234567890L)</jc>
416    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
417    *    <jk>long</jk> <jv>timestamp</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getLong(<js>"timestamp"</js>).orElse(0L);  <jc>// Returns 1234567890L</jc>
418    * </p>
419    *
420    * @param methodName The method name.
421    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as a long, or empty if not found or not a {@code long} type.
422    */
423   public Optional<Long> getLong(String methodName) {
424      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(long.class)).map(x -> (Long)x.invoke(a));
425   }
426
427   /**
428    * Returns the method with the specified name on this annotation.
429    *
430    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
431    * <p class='bjava'>
432    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
433    *    Optional&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>method</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getMethod(<js>"value"</js>);
434    *    <jv>method</jv>.ifPresent(<jv>m</jv> -&gt; System.<jsf>out</jsf>.println(<jv>m</jv>.getReturnType()));
435    * </p>
436    *
437    * @param methodName The method name to look for.
438    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the method info, or empty if method not found.
439    */
440   public Optional<MethodInfo> getMethod(String methodName) {
441      return methods.get().stream().filter(x -> eq(methodName, x.getNameSimple())).findFirst();
442   }
443
444   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
445   // Private helper methods
446   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
447
448   /**
449    * Returns the class name of this annotation.
450    *
451    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
452    * <p class='bjava'>
453    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
454    *    String <jv>name</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getName();  <jc>// Returns "mypackage.MyAnnotation"</jc>
455    * </p>
456    *
457    * @return The simple class name of the annotation (e.g., {@code "Override"} for {@code @Override}).
458    */
459   public String getName() { return cn(a.annotationType()); }
460
461   /**
462    * Returns the simple class name of this annotation.
463    *
464    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
465    * <p class='bjava'>
466    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
467    *    String <jv>name</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getNameSimple();  <jc>// Returns "MyAnnotation"</jc>
468    * </p>
469    *
470    * @return The simple class name of the annotation (e.g., {@code "Override"} for {@code @Override}).
471    * @see #getName()
472    */
473   public String getNameSimple() { return cns(a.annotationType()); }
474
475   /**
476    * Returns the rank of this annotation for sorting by precedence.
477    *
478    * <p>
479    * The rank is determined by checking if the annotation has a {@code rank()} method that returns an {@code int}.
480    * If found, that value is used; otherwise the rank defaults to {@code 0}.
481    *
482    * <p>
483    * Higher rank values indicate higher precedence when multiple annotations of the same type are present.
484    *
485    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
486    * <p class='bjava'>
487    *    <jc>// Annotation with rank method</jc>
488    *    <ja>@interface</ja> MyAnnotation {
489    *       <jk>int</jk> rank() <jk>default</jk> 0;
490    *    }
491    *
492    *    <jc>// Get rank from annotation info</jc>
493    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
494    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>rank</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getRank();  <jc>// Returns value from rank() method</jc>
495    * </p>
496    *
497    * @return The rank of this annotation, or {@code 0} if no rank method exists.
498    */
499   public int getRank() { return rank; }
500
501   /**
502    * Returns the return type of the specified method on this annotation.
503    *
504    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
505    * <p class='bjava'>
506    *    Optional&lt;ClassInfo&gt; <jv>returnType</jv> = <jv>annotationInfo</jv>.getReturnType(<js>"value"</js>);
507    * </p>
508    *
509    * @param methodName The method name.
510    * @return An optional containing the return type of the specified method, or empty if method not found.
511    */
512   public Optional<ClassInfo> getReturnType(String methodName) {
513      return getMethod(methodName).map(x -> x.getReturnType());
514   }
515
516   /**
517    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a string.
518    *
519    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
520    * <p class='bjava'>
521    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(name="John", age=30)</jc>
522    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
523    *    String <jv>name</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getString(<js>"name"</js>).orElse(<js>"unknown"</js>);  <jc>// Returns "John"</jc>
524    * </p>
525    *
526    * @param methodName The method name.
527    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value as a string, or empty if not found or not a string type.
528    */
529   public Optional<String> getString(String methodName) {
530      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(String.class)).map(x -> s(x.invoke(a)));
531   }
532
533   /**
534    * Returns the value of the specified method on this annotation as a string array.
535    *
536    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
537    * <p class='bjava'>
538    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation(tags={"foo", "bar"})</jc>
539    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
540    *    String[] <jv>tags</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getStringArray(<js>"tags"</js>).orElse(<jk>new</jk> String[0]);  <jc>// Returns ["foo", "bar"]</jc>
541    * </p>
542    *
543    * @param methodName The method name.
544    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the string array value, or empty if not found or not a {@code String[]} type.
545    */
546   public Optional<String[]> getStringArray(String methodName) {
547      return getMethod(methodName).filter(x -> x.hasReturnType(String[].class)).map(x -> (String[])x.invoke(a));
548   }
549
550   /**
551    * Returns the value of the {@code value()} method on this annotation as a string.
552    *
553    * <p>
554    * This is a convenience method equivalent to calling {@link #getString(String) getString("value")}.
555    *
556    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
557    * <p class='bjava'>
558    *    <jc>// For annotation: @MyAnnotation("foo")</jc>
559    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
560    *    String <jv>value</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getValue().orElse(<js>"default"</js>);  <jc>// Returns "foo"</jc>
561    * </p>
562    *
563    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value of the {@code value()} method, or empty if not found or not a string.
564    */
565   public Optional<String> getValue() { return getString("value"); }
566
567   /**
568    * Returns the value of a specific annotation method.
569    *
570    * <p>
571    * This method provides type-safe access to annotation field values without requiring
572    * explicit reflection calls or casting.
573    *
574    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
575    * <p class='bjava'>
576    *    <jc>// For annotation: @interface MyAnnotation { String value(); int priority(); }</jc>
577    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
578    *
579    *    <jc>// Get string value</jc>
580    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>value</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getValue(String.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"value"</js>);
581    *
582    *    <jc>// Get int value</jc>
583    *    Optional&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>priority</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.getValue(Integer.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"priority"</js>);
584    * </p>
585    *
586    * @param <V> The expected type of the annotation field value.
587    * @param type The expected class of the annotation field value.
588    * @param name The name of the annotation method (field).
589    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the value if found and type matches, empty otherwise.
590    */
591   @SuppressWarnings({
592      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to V for annotation value
593   })
594   public <V> Optional<V> getValue(Class<V> type, String name) {
595      // @formatter:off
596      return methods.get().stream()
597         .filter(m -> eq(m.getName(), name) && eq(m.getReturnType().inner(), type))
598         .map(m -> safe(() -> (V)m.invoke(a)))
599         .findFirst();
600      // @formatter:on
601   }
602
603   /**
604    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this annotation is itself annotated with the specified annotation.
605    *
606    * <p>
607    * This checks for meta-annotations on the annotation type.
608    *
609    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
610    * <p class='bjava'>
611    *    <jc>// Check if @MyAnnotation is annotated with @Documented</jc>
612    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
613    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>isDocumented</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.hasAnnotation(Documented.<jk>class</jk>);
614    * </p>
615    *
616    * @param <A> The meta-annotation type.
617    * @param type The meta-annotation to test for.
618    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this annotation is annotated with the specified annotation.
619    */
620   public <A extends Annotation> boolean hasAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
621      return nn(this.a.annotationType().getAnnotation(type));
622   }
623
624   /**
625    * Returns the hash code of this annotation.
626    *
627    * <p>
628    * Same as calling {@link Annotation#hashCode()} on the wrapped annotation.
629    *
630    * <p>
631    * The hash code of an annotation is the sum of the hash codes of its members (including those with default values).
632    *
633    * @return The hash code of this annotation.
634    * @see Annotation#hashCode()
635    */
636   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
637   public int hashCode() {
638      return a.hashCode();
639   }
640
641   /**
642    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this annotation has the specified fully-qualified name.
643    *
644    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
645    * <p class='bjava'>
646    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>isName</jv> = <jv>annotationInfo</jv>.hasName(<js>"org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.Name"</js>);
647    * </p>
648    *
649    * @param value The fully-qualified name to check.
650    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this annotation has the specified fully-qualified name.
651    */
652   public boolean hasName(String value) {
653      return eq(value, getName());
654   }
655
656   /**
657    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this annotation has the specified simple name.
658    *
659    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
660    * <p class='bjava'>
661    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>isName</jv> = <jv>annotationInfo</jv>.hasSimpleName(<js>"Name"</js>);
662    * </p>
663    *
664    * @param value The simple name to check.
665    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this annotation has the specified simple name.
666    */
667   public boolean hasNameSimple(String value) {
668      return eq(value, getNameSimple());
669   }
670
671   /**
672    * Returns the wrapped annotation instance.
673    *
674    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
675    * <p class='bjava'>
676    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
677    *    MyAnnotation <jv>annotation</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.inner();
678    *
679    *    <jc>// Access annotation methods directly</jc>
680    *    String <jv>value</jv> = <jv>annotation</jv>.value();
681    * </p>
682    *
683    * @return The wrapped annotation instance.
684    */
685   public T inner() {
686      return a;
687   }
688
689   /**
690    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this annotation is in the specified {@link AnnotationGroup}.
691    *
692    * <p>
693    * Annotation groups are used to logically group related annotations together.
694    * This checks if the annotation is annotated with {@link AnnotationGroup} and if
695    * the group value matches the specified type.
696    *
697    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
698    * <p class='bjava'>
699    *    <jc>// Define an annotation group</jc>
700    *    <ja>@interface</ja> MyGroup {}
701    *
702    *    <jc>// Annotation in the group</jc>
703    *    <ja>@AnnotationGroup</ja>(MyGroup.<jk>class</jk>)
704    *    <ja>@interface</ja> MyAnnotation {}
705    *
706    *    <jc>// Check if annotation is in group</jc>
707    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
708    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>inGroup</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.isInGroup(MyGroup.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// Returns true</jc>
709    * </p>
710    *
711    * @param <A> The group annotation type.
712    * @param group The group annotation class to test for.
713    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this annotation is in the specified group.
714    * @see AnnotationGroup
715    */
716   public <A extends Annotation> boolean isInGroup(Class<A> group) {
717      var x = a.annotationType().getAnnotation(AnnotationGroup.class);
718      return (nn(x) && x.value().equals(group));
719   }
720
721   /**
722    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this annotation is of the specified type.
723    *
724    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
725    * <p class='bjava'>
726    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;?&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
727    *
728    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>ai</jv>.isType(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>)) {
729    *       <jc>// Handle MyAnnotation specifically</jc>
730    *    }
731    * </p>
732    *
733    * @param <A> The annotation type to test for.
734    * @param type The annotation type to test against.
735    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this annotation's type is exactly the specified type.
736    */
737   public <A extends Annotation> boolean isType(Class<A> type) {
738      return this.a.annotationType() == type;
739   }
740
741   /**
742    * Converts this annotation info to a map representation for debugging purposes.
743    *
744    * <p>
745    * The returned map contains:
746    * <ul>
747    *    <li>The annotatable element's type and label (e.g., {@code "CLASS_TYPE" -> "com.example.MyClass"})
748    *    <li>A nested map with the annotation's simple name as key and its non-default values
749    * </ul>
750    *
751    * <p>
752    * Only annotation values that differ from their default values are included.
753    *
754    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
755    * <p class='bjava'>
756    *    AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt; <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
757    *    LinkedHashMap&lt;String,Object&gt; <jv>map</jv> = <jv>ai</jv>.toMap();
758    *    <jc>// Returns: {"CLASS_TYPE": "MyClass", "@MyAnnotation": {"value": "foo", "priority": 5}}</jc>
759    * </p>
760    *
761    * @return A new map showing the attributes of this annotation info.
762    */
763   public FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
764      // @formatter:off
765      var ca = info(a.annotationType());
766      var ja = mapBuilder().sorted().buildFluent();  // NOAI
767      ca.getDeclaredMethods().forEach(x -> safeOptCatch(() -> {
768         var val = x.invoke(a);
769         var d = x.inner().getDefaultValue();
770         // Add values only if they're different from the default.
771         if (neq(val, d) && ! (isArray(val) && length(val) == 0 && isArray(d) && length(d) == 0))
772            return val;
773         return null;
774      }, e -> localizedMessage(e)).ifPresent(v -> ja.a(x.getName(), v)));
775      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
776         .a(s(annotatable.getAnnotatableType()), annotatable.getLabel())
777         .a("@" + ca.getNameSimple(), ja);
778      // @formatter:on
779   }
780
781   /**
782    * Returns a simple string representation of this annotation showing the annotation type and location.
783    *
784    * <p>
785    * Format: {@code @AnnotationName(on=location)}
786    *
787    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
788    * <ul>
789    *    <li>{@code @Marshalled(on=MyClass)} - Annotation on a class
790    *    <li>{@code @Inject(on=MyClass.myField)} - Annotation on a field
791    *    <li>{@code @PackageAnnotation(on=my.package)} - Annotation on a package
792    * </ul>
793    *
794    * @return A simple string representation of this annotation.
795    */
796   public String toSimpleString() {
797      return "@" + cns(a.annotationType()) + "(on=" + annotatable.getLabel() + ")";
798   }
799
800   /**
801    * Returns a detailed string representation of this annotation.
802    *
803    * <p>
804    * The returned string includes:
805    * <ul>
806    *    <li>Annotation type (fully qualified name with @ prefix)
807    *    <li>Location where the annotation is declared (on=...)
808    *    <li>Annotation values (non-default values only, in key=value format)
809    * </ul>
810    *
811    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
812    * <p class='bjava'>
813    *    <jc>// Simple annotation on a class</jc>
814    *    AnnotationInfo <jv>ai</jv> = ...;
815    *    <jv>ai</jv>.toString();
816    *    <jc>// Returns: "@java.lang.Deprecated(on=org.example.MyClass)"</jc>
817    *
818    *    <jc>// Annotation with value</jc>
819    *    <jc>// Returns: "@org.example.MyAnnotation(value=foo, on=org.example.MyClass)"</jc>
820    *
821    *    <jc>// Annotation on a method</jc>
822    *    <jc>// Returns: "@org.example.RestGet(path=/api, on=org.example.MyClass.myMethod)"</jc>
823    *
824    *    <jc>// Annotation on a field</jc>
825    *    <jc>// Returns: "@org.example.Inject(name=myBean, on=org.example.MyClass.myField)"</jc>
826    * </p>
827    *
828    * <h5 class='section'>Comparison with Other Methods:</h5>
829    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
830    *    <li>{@link #toSimpleString()} - Returns simple format: "@AnnotationName(on=location)"
831    *    <li>{@link #properties()} - Returns map representation for debugging
832    * </ul>
833    *
834    * @return A detailed string representation including annotation type, location, and values.
835    */
836   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
837   public String toString() {
838      return toString.get();
839   }
840
841   private String findToString() {
842      var sb = new StringBuilder(256);
843
844      // Annotation type (with @ prefix and full package name)
845      sb.append("@");
846      var annotationType = a.annotationType();
847      ClassInfo.of(annotationType).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
848
849      // Get annotation values (non-default only)
850      var ca = info(annotationType);
851      var values = new ArrayList<String>();
852      ca.getDeclaredMethods().forEach(m -> safeOptCatch(() -> {
853         var val = m.invoke(a);
854         var d = m.inner().getDefaultValue();
855         // Add values only if they're different from the default
856         if (neq(val, d) && ! (isArray(val) && length(val) == 0 && isArray(d) && length(d) == 0)) {
857            var valueStr = formatAnnotationValue(val);
858            values.add(m.getName() + "=" + valueStr);
859         }
860         return null;
861      }, e -> null));
862
863      // Build the string: @AnnotationType(key1=value1, key2=value2, on=location)
864      if (! values.isEmpty()) {
865         sb.append("(");
866         sb.append(String.join(", ", values));
867         sb.append(", on=");
868      } else {
869         sb.append("(on=");
870      }
871      sb.append(annotatable.getLabel());
872      sb.append(")");
873
874      return sb.toString();
875   }
876
877   private String formatAnnotationValue(Object value) {
878      if (value == null)
879         return "null";
880      if (value instanceof String)
881         return "\"" + value + "\"";
882      if (isArray(value)) {
883         var array = toList(value, Object.class);
884         if (array.isEmpty())
885            return "{}";
886         var elements = array.stream()
887            .map(this::formatAnnotationValue)
888            .collect(java.util.stream.Collectors.joining(", "));
889         return "{" + elements + "}";
890      }
891      if (value instanceof Class<?> c) {
892         var sb = new StringBuilder();
893         ClassInfo.of(c).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
894         return sb.toString() + ".class";
895      }
896      if (value instanceof Enum<?> e) {
897         return e.getDeclaringClass().getSimpleName() + "." + e.name();
898      }
899      if (value instanceof Annotation value2) {
900         // For nested annotations, use simple format
901         return "@" + ClassInfo.of(value2.annotationType()).getNameSimple() + "(...)";
902      }
903      return String.valueOf(value);
904   }
905}
